name: Location Tracker
note:
Tracks the current location from a structured header (📍 Location) written by the LLM each turn. Detects changes, upserts lorebook entries in trg_utility, and hands off to VLZ (vlz-discover / vlz-inject-location). Requires a trg_utility lorebook. Replace the call-llm connection ID with your own.

Normalization: leading articles (the, a, an) are stripped from extracted location names so 'The Tavern' and 'Tavern' are treated as the same location.

Fuzzy routing: the known/new split uses {{fuzzy:}} (Jaro-Winkler, threshold 80) over the list of known lorebook titles so minor LLM phrasing drift still resolves to the correct stored entry.

Rule order is significant: 'Detect location change' and 'No loc header' both set LT-changed and LT-lb_entry; the VLZ routing rules ('New location', 'Known location') come after both so they see whichever path ran.

rules.name: Chat loaded — report current location
rules.note: Toasts the persisted location on chat load so you can see what state you're resuming into.

rules.name: Extract full location
rules.note: Captures everything after 📍 up to ]. Strips leading articles (the/a/an) so stored titles are article-free.

rules.name: Extract parent location
rules.note: Captures only the parent portion before ' - ', or the whole string if no sub-location. Strips leading articles.

rules.name: Detect location change
rules.note: Compares the freshly extracted loc against the persisted chatvar. Uses {{fuzzy:}} over the list of known lorebook titles to find the canonical stored title — if a fuzzy match exists, LT-lb_entry is non-empty and downstream routes to the known-location path.

rules.name: Upsert parent location entry
rules.note: Fires on any location change. update is an upsert keyed on title — safe to call unconditionally.

rules.name: No loc header — generate and prepend
rules.note: Fallback for first turn or when the LLM omits the header. Generates one, prepends it, then normalizes and fuzzy-routes the same way as the normal path. Must come before 'New location' and 'Known location' so those rules see LT-changed and LT-lb_entry from either path.

actions.prompt:
[System Note: Generate a header reflecting the conditions at the end of the CURRENT TURN.]

---

### CONTEXT
HISTORY: 
{{history:1}}

CURRENT TURN: 
{{message}}

KNOWN LOCATIONS:
{{lbTitles:trg_utility::AND({{chat_id}}, location):all:inactive}}

---

### RULES
Analyze the HISTORY and CURRENT TURN to determine the state of each variable. If information is missing, invent a suitable entry:

1. **TimeOfDay, Date, & Era**: Maintain consistency with the prior turn's header, adjusting logically if time progressed.
2. **Location**: Match against KNOWN LOCATIONS exactly. If not found, create a new one. Use `Parent - Sub` format (e.g., `Bostaff's home - Kitchen`) or just the singular name if no specific room is defined (e.g., `The Smoking Pipe`).
3. **Present**: Named characters physically in the room at the end of the CURRENT TURN, excluding {{user}} and any unnamed or background characters.

---

### OUTPUT FORMAT
Output ONLY the formatted header. No markdown blocks, no preamble, no filler.

[ TimeOfDay | 🗓️ DayOfWeek, Month DD, YYYY Era | 📍 Location ]
Present: Named characters only, excluding {{user}}

rules.name: New location — create LB entry and discover
rules.note: Fires only when {{fuzzy:}} found no match for this location. Creates the entry and hands off to VLZ. Comes after both 'Detect location change' and 'No loc header' so LT-lb_entry is set by whichever path ran.

rules.name: Known location — inject into VLZ
rules.note: Fires when {{fuzzy:}} returned a match. Passes the canonical stored title to VLZ, skipping discovery.

rules.name: Persist current location to chatvar
rules.note: Fires whenever LT-current_loc is set this turn, regardless of which path produced it.

rules.name: Update location preset
rules.note: Fires every turn when LT-parent_loc is set. Composes and injects the location context preset.

actions.template:
<locations>
Known locations:
{{lbTitles:trg_utility::AND({{chat_id}}, location, parent):all:inactive}}

{{LT-parent_loc}} sub-locations:
{{lbTitles:trg_utility::AND({{chat_id}}, location, {{LT-parent_loc}}, !parent):all:inactive}}

When writing the location header, match against known locations exactly. Use Parent - Sub format. If the location is not listed, create a new one. The location must reflect where the scene ends at the conclusion of the current turn, not where it began. Present should list named characters only, excluding {{user}} and any unnamed or background characters.
</locations>
